tweets.text: RT @AutismCapital: 🚨FYI: Gavin Newsom has already signed California Assembly Bill 1043 which mandates that operating systems including Wind…

retweet.text:
🚨FYI: Gavin Newsom has already signed California Assembly Bill 1043 which mandates that operating systems including Windows, Linux, macOS, and FreeBSD implement system-level age (identity) verification.

This takes effect on January 1st, 2027.

Colorado also has a similar act called SB26-051 which is currently advancing through the committees and ready for full Senate consideration. It would come into effect on January 1st, 2028 if passed.

Utah, Texas, Louisiana, and Alabama all currently have app layer verification laws, but not OS level YET. 

So OS level verification isn't a future thing. It's happening now. They are chokepointing the internet.

Enjoy these days.

tweets.text: Tunnels are so underrated

quoted.text:
The real story is the $25 million per mile price tag they’re betting on.

Nashville’s own 2018 light rail plan priced at $200 million per mile. New York’s East Side Access cost $3.5 billion per mile. The LA Metro expansion is running $1 billion per mile. The Boring Company says it can build 13 miles of twin tunnels through Nashville for $240-300 million total.

That’s a 95% cost reduction from the industry average. If the number holds, it rewrites the economics of every transit project in America. If it doesn’t, a few hundred million in private capital evaporates and taxpayers lose nothing. That risk asymmetry explains why Tennessee said yes when LA, Chicago, Baltimore, and DC all said no.

The engineering gamble is wild. 12-foot diameter tunnels instead of 28-foot. Fully electric Prufrock machines that mine continuously instead of stopping every 5 feet to install lining segments. Zero people in the tunnel during operations. A machine that “porpoises” into the ground from a truck instead of requiring million-dollar launch pits and cranes.

Every one of those innovations has worked in Las Vegas sand. None have been tested in karst limestone, the geology that creates sinkholes, caves, and underground streams. Their own CEO said at the unveiling that Nashville would not be their choice if they were optimizing for easiest places to tunnel.

This tells you everything about what The Boring Company is actually trying to prove. Nashville is where the thesis meets the hardest possible geology. 50 inches of annual rainfall versus Vegas’s 4. Rock that creates underground caves and streams. They just signed a construction contract in Dubai too, meaning they need Nashville to work before the next project launches.

The internal memo from the governor’s office estimates 1 mile per month. The Boring Company’s website claims 1 mile per week. That 4x gap between political planning and corporate marketing will determine whether this finishes in 2027 or 2030.

Week 7, when Prufrock-MB2 arrives, is when this gets real. Two machines boring simultaneously through Tennessee limestone will answer the question the entire tunneling industry has been debating for a decade: whether a startup can actually outrun the physics that made infrastructure the slowest-moving sector in construction.

tweets.text:
RT @kevinnbass: THE LARRY SUMMERS FILES

Larry Summers was the economic architect of the Clinton presidency -- Deputy Treasury Secretary, t…

retweet.text:
THE LARRY SUMMERS FILES

Larry Summers was the economic architect of the Clinton presidency -- Deputy Treasury Secretary, then Treasury Secretary, one of the most powerful men in Washington. Clinton trusted him with the global financial system.

He was the Secretary of the Treasury. He was the President of Harvard. He sat on the board of OpenAI.

He was one of the most influential economists alive.

He says his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein was "a major error of judgement."

739 documents say it was a 14-year partnership.

He says he "deeply regrets being in contact with Epstein after his conviction."

"Contact."

The documents show 55 confirmed meetings across 14 locations over 14 years. New York. Cambridge. Palm Beach. Kazakhstan. Davos. Paris. Saudi Arabia. At Epstein's 71st Street mansion at least 20 times.

Epstein at Summers' home in Brookline at least 4 times.

55 meetings is not "contact." 55 meetings is a relationship.

Summers says he had "no involvement in [Epstein's] financial matters."

The documents show Epstein was Summers' de facto business agent.

Epstein set Summers' consulting fees. He told the Turkish central bank: $250,000. Summers asked: "R they stooges of Erdogan?"

Epstein arranged the Kazakhstan engagement. "Kazakhs paid!"

Epstein processed payments through his offshore entity in the Virgin Islands. When Summers had travel expenses, his assistant was told: "Jeffrey says to invoice STC!" -- Southern Trust Company, St. Thomas, USVI.

Meanwhile, Summers -- the former Secretary of the Treasury -- was advising Epstein on tax avoidance:

"If an American with 1 billion showed up in your office aged 55 wanting to live on 100m, give 100m to charity, wanting to get rest of 800m thru to his kids, how little tax could you enable him to pay?"

A former Treasury Secretary asking a convicted sex offender how to minimize estate taxes on $800 million.

Summers says he had "absolutely no knowledge" that he was named in Epstein's will.

Epstein named Summers SUCCESSOR EXECUTOR of his estate -- the person entrusted with managing everything if the primary executors couldn't serve. The will was signed November 18, 2014. Summers was advising Epstein on the $800M estate transfer at the same time.

You don't name a casual acquaintance as executor of your estate. And you don't advise someone on their $800 million tax strategy without knowing you're in their will.

Summers' wife Elisa New ("Lisa") developed her own independent financial relationship with Epstein. Her nonprofit Verse Video Education received $220,000+ from Epstein entities -- $110,000 from the Epstein Foundation and $110,000 from Gratitude America, an Epstein shell.

When asked who the donor should be listed as, Epstein said: "the supporter is me, no name necessary."

Lisa flew on Epstein's private jet to Palm Beach with Woody Allen and Soon-Yi. She wrote to Epstein afterward: "What fun with woody and soon yi! Up there with Gordon Brown and King Hussein."

She sent annual thank-you letters: "your amazing support without which none of this would have happened. Larry tells me you are the smartest person he knows."

The spokesman says Summers' wife's nonprofit "regrets accepting funding from Epstein." Accepting. As if it landed in her lap. She solicited it with detailed budgets over multiple years.

Epstein actively concealed the Summers relationship. When Leon Black asked for Epstein's Harvard schedule, Epstein told his staff: "yes but leave off summers."

The concealment was mutual. In November 2018 -- 48 hours after the Miami Herald published its devastating "Perversion of Justice" investigation -- Summers attended dinner at Epstein's 71st Street mansion. His message: "Not helpful to have my office involved. U around this afternoon. New worry."

Two days after an expose about Epstein's sex trafficking, Summers was dining at the man's home while hiding the meeting from his own staff.

The relationship didn't slow down. It intensified.

After the Herald expose, Epstein became Summers' relationship counselor. Summers was pursuing a woman he described as a mentee. Epstein coached him through it -- 21-page email chains, ghostwritten apology messages.

Epstein to Summers: "im a pretty good wing man, no? anymore how great larry is comments and i would have had to barf."

Summers to Epstein: "She was gorgeous. I'm fucked."

This continued through the spring of 2019. Dinner at 71st Street in April, 82 days before arrest. When Summers had emergency surgery in Antigua, his assistant contacted Epstein as one of Larry's "close friends."

The last documented exchange was an iMessage chain spanning June 14 to July 5, 2019 -- three weeks before Epstein's arrest on July 6. Epstein was still arranging Davos access: "davos is set for you." Summers was still offering political intelligence: "being asked for 3 names to replace mnuchin."

Epstein's pilot testified that Lawrence Summers was on the plane "while young girls were present."

Summers made 7 public statements about his Epstein relationship between 2023 and 2026. I extracted 12 testable claims.

0 rated TRUE.

7 rated FALSE. 3 rated MISLEADING. 1 CONTESTED. 1 UNADDRESSED.

The defense evolved in five phases:

1. Silence (2019-2023).
2. Spokesperson buffer ("deeply regrets being in contact").
3. Personal statement ("deeply ashamed," steps back from all public roles).
4. Will denial ("absolutely no knowledge").
5. Silent exit (Harvard resignation, no mention of Epstein).

Each phase collapsed when new documents surfaced.

He resigned from OpenAI. From Bloomberg. From Yale Budget Lab. From the Hamilton Project. From the Center for American Progress. From Harvard itself -- ending a 50-year career.

He has never addressed the financial agency arrangement. He has never addressed the concealment. He has never explained how a convicted sex offender became his business agent, his relationship counselor, and the man he trusted with his estate.

1,424 documents were reviewed. 685 were rejected. Nothing was cherry-picked.

Every document is referenced by ID and linked to the original DOJ source.

Full compendium (739 docs, 238 pages): https://t.co/joNEYBSOrk

AI-optimized file (upload to any LLM and ask it anything): https://t.co/doZ7i43LRd

After uploading the file to AI, here's a list of questions to get started:

What are the 5 most important documents in this file? For each, give the EFTA key, date, and quote the key passage verbatim.

Summers says his relationship with Epstein was "a major error of judgement." How many documents span how many years? Is that one error or a pattern?

What exactly was the financial relationship between Summers and Epstein? Trace every payment, invoice, and consulting arrangement. Who paid whom, for what, and through which entities?

What role did Lisa (Elisa New) play? How much money flowed to her nonprofit, from which Epstein entities, and was she an active or passive participant?

What happened in the 48 hours after the Miami Herald published its Epstein investigation? Quote the exact texts.

Trace the Keyu Jin "wing man" thread from start to finish. What was Epstein's role and what did Summers tell him?

What did Epstein's pilot testify about Summers? What does the flight log show?

List every public claim Larry Summers has made about Epstein and rate each one as true or false based on the documentary evidence in this file.

Who are the 30 brokered connections documented in the network file? Name them and describe what Epstein arranged.

If you were a congressional investigator, what are the 3 most important questions you would ask Larry Summers under oath? Cite specific EFTA keys.

Write a 500-word executive summary of the Summers-Epstein relationship for someone who knows nothing about it. Cite specific documents.

Happy chatting.

See ya, Larry.

tweets.text: RT @SERobinsonJr: STARLINK: Virgin Media O2 has officially launched their Starlink powered O2 Satellite, the first satellite-powered mobile…

retweet.text:
STARLINK: Virgin Media O2 has officially launched their Starlink powered O2 Satellite, the first satellite-powered mobile data service in the UK.

The service uses Starlink Direct to Cell and O2’s licensed spectrum to deliver connectivity directly to mobile devices. It boosts UK landmass coverage from 89% to 95%. 

Per Stephanie Bednarek, VP of Starlink Commercial Sales, "Delivering Starlink Direct to Cell in partnership with Virgin Media O2 underscores the importance of keeping people connected no matter where they are. For the first time, millions of people across the UK will have access to data, voice and video through apps, and messaging in remote areas where terrestrial coverage isn’t available."

It starts as a £3-per-month add-on, available initially on the latest Samsung smartphones, with plans to expand device and app support soon. Ultimate Plan customers will receive it at no extra cost in the near future.

tweets.text:
RT @AISafetyMemes: Anthropic CEO: next year, AI models may “able to replicate and survive in the wild”

DARIO AMODEI: Yeah, I think A.S.L.…

retweet.text:
Anthropic CEO: next year, AI models may “able to replicate and survive in the wild”

DARIO AMODEI: Yeah, I think A.S.L. [AI Safety Level] 3 could easily happen this year or next year. I think A.S.L. 4 —

EZRA KLEIN: Oh, Jesus Christ.

DARIO: No, no, I told you. I’m a believer in exponentials. I think A.S.L. 4 could happen anywhere from 2025 to 2028.

EZRA: So that is fast.

DARIO: Yeah, no, no, I’m truly talking about the near future here.

…

EZRA KLEIN: What do you specify A.S.L. 3 as? What are you implying A.S.L. 4 is?

DARIO AMODEI: A.S.L. 3 is triggered by risks related to misuse of biology and cyber technology. A.S.L. 4, we’re working on now.

… so for example, on biology, the way we’ve defined it — and we’re still refining the test, but the way we’ve defined it is, relative to use of a Google search, there’s a substantial increase in risk as would be evaluated by, say, the national security community of misuse of biology, creation of bioweapons, that either the proliferation or spread of it is greater than it was before, or the capabilities are substantially greater than it was before.

We’ll probably have some more exact quantitative thing, working with folks who are ex-government biodefense folks, but something like this accounts for 20 percent of the total source of risk of biological attacks, or something increases the risk by 20 percent or something like that. So that would be a very concrete version of it. It’s just, it takes us time to develop very concrete criteria. So that would be like A.S.L. 3.

A.S.L. 4 is going to be more about, on the misuse side, enabling state-level actors to greatly increase their capability, which is much harder than enabling random people. So where we would worry that North Korea or China or Russia could greatly enhance their offensive capabilities in various military areas with A.I. in a way that would give them a substantial advantage at the geopolitical level. And on the autonomy side, it’s various measures of these models are pretty close to being able to replicate and survive in the wild.

So it feels maybe one step short of models that would, I think, raise truly existential questions."

A silent filibuster allows a 40-senator minority to block a bill *without even debating*

Literally, they can block a bill by sleeping. 

Luckily, @LeaderJohnThune  can simply require debate (talking filibuster), and eventually the bill will go to a vote and pass. 

So, if he refuses to do this, he is co-signing defeat.

Not just abstract defeat. Defeat of the will of 84% of Americans, and the demise of secure elections. 

This is unacceptable. 

There is no greater priority than secure elections.

tweets.text: RT @YossiBenYakar: An 82-year-old grandmother in Vienna, suffering from dementia, was brutally raped by her care nurse—a 26-year-old Afghan…

retweet.text:
An 82-year-old grandmother in Vienna, suffering from dementia, was brutally raped by her care nurse—a 26-year-old Afghan migrant.

Her son had to endure the ultimate nightmare: watching the assault happen in real-time on his smartphone through a security camera he installed out of concern.

This is what happens when a state prioritizes mass migration over the safety of its most vulnerable citizens. When you import the Third World, you import a complete disregard for Western values and basic human decency.

Austria's elders are now prey in their own homes. The system didn't just fail this woman; it invited her predator in.

Is there any level of horror that will finally force European governments to prioritize their own people over illegal migrants?

tweets.text: RT @SawyerMerritt: Based on my research, more than 5,700 commercial aircraft either already have SpaceX’s @Starlink installed or are under…

retweet.text:
Based on my research, more than 5,700 commercial aircraft either already have SpaceX’s @Starlink installed or are under contract to receive it. That would account for ~19% of the active global commercial aircraft fleet when all installs are completed.

SpaceX has already gotten two of the top four largest U.S. airlines to adopt Starlink (United and Southwest).

List of airlines/operators that have publicly announced Starlink internet adoption for their fleet:
• Southwest Airlines
• United Airlines
• British Airways
• Emirates
• Qatar Airlines
• Air France
• Hawaiian Airlines
• Alaska Airlines
• Virgin Atlantic
• Lufthansa
• Korean air
• Air Baltic
• Aer Lingus
• Air Busan
• Air Dolomiti
• Air New Zealand
• Air Seoul
• Asiana Airlines
• Austrian Airlines
• Swiss Airlines
• Scandinavian Airlines
• Gulf Air
• Iberia
• Discover Airlines
• flydubai
• ITA Airways
• Vueling
• Brussels Airlines
• Jin Air
• LEVEL
• WestJet
• Edelweiss Air
• JSX
• ZipAir
• Eurowings

And all this doesn't even include business jets. More than 1,000 business jets are currently equipped with Starlink, representing more than 50% of all active tails. Starlink's aviation division is and will no doubt continue to see massive growth.
